are interested in studying Earth systems. This major provides a fundamental background and solid understanding of physical, chemical, and biological processes that shape the earth. Coursework includes petrology and mineralogy, sedimentology, structural geology and tectonophysics, seismology, isotope geochemistry, geomorphology, and the Earth's interior.</span></p><p><br></p><p>Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major.</p><p><br></p><p>To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer:</p><ul><li>One introductory course in Earth Sciences with Lab,</li><li>One year of General Chemistry with Lab, and</li><li>One semester of Calculus.</li></ul><p>Completion of a  second semester of Calculus is <strong>STRONGLY RECOMMENDED</strong> before transfer in the fall.</p><p>All courses must be taken for a letter grade.</p><p><br></p><p>For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit <a href=\"http://www.ess.ucla.edu\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://epss.ucla.edu</a> and <u style=\"color: rgb(41, 98, 255);\">https://</u><a href=\"http://www.admision.ucla edu.\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">admission.ucla ed</a><u style=\"color: rgb(41, 98, 255);\">u</u>.</p><p><br></p><p>PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.</p>","groupId":"64c26f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"}]
